zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth Land於1815年6月10日出生在美國南卡羅來納州斯帕爾坦波格斯帕爾坦波格時，她的父親Lewis Land56歲，母親Elizabeth Martha Ray54歲。她於1831年7月15日與John Bailey在美國南卡羅來納州結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她於1870年住在美國阿肯色州蘭多夫洛亞諾可 堂史普且於1879年住在美國阿肯色州拉法葉瓦爾努特 希爾。她去世於1890年3月16日在美國阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯享年74歲，埋葬在美國阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯真斯 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth Land于1815年6月10日出生在美国南卡罗来纳州斯帕爾坦波格斯帕爾坦波格时，她的父亲Lewis Land56岁，母亲Elizabeth Martha Ray54岁。她于1831年7月15日与John Bailey在美国南卡罗来纳州结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她于1870年住在美国阿肯色州蘭多夫洛亞諾可 堂史普且于1879年住在美国阿肯色州拉法葉瓦爾努特 希爾。她去世于1890年3月16日在美国阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯享年74岁，埋葬在美国阿肯色州勞倫斯拉文登 斯普林斯真斯 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Land
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, and Dutch: topographic name from Old English, Middle Dutch land, Middle High German lant ‘land, territory’. This had more specialized senses in the Middle Ages, being used to denote the countryside as opposed to a town or an estate.
English: topographic name from Middle English launde ‘glade’ (Old French land), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Launde in Leicestershire or Laund in Yorkshire.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of the three farmsteads so named, from Old Norse land ‘land, territory’ (see 1 above).

variants.name: Lander
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic or status name from Middle High German lant, German Land ‘land, territory’ (see Land 1). Compare Landmann .
German: topographic name from Middle High German lander ‘picket fence’.
German: from the personal name Lanther, see Landherr 1.

variants.name: Lands
meanings: English: variant of   Land  .

variants.name: Landt
meanings: German: variant of   Land   1.

variants.name: Lande
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads, so named from either the dative singular or the plural form of land (see Land 3).
German: from the old personal name Lando, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with lant ‘land’ (see Land ).

French: topographic name for someone living on a heath, moor, French lande (from Gaulish landa ‘space, land’), or a habitational name from any of numerous minor places called La Lande from this word. Compare Lalande .

variants.name: Landfried
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Chantel.
South German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements land ‘land, territory’ (see Land ) + fred, frid ‘peace’. This surname is also found in France (Lorraine).

variants.name: Lawn
meanings: English:
variant of   Land   2.
from Middle English laun ‘lawn, fine linen’ (French laun, from the name of the city of Laon), perhaps for a maker or trader.

variants.name: Landa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jorge, Jose, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Enrique, Jesus, Julio, Mario, Roberto, Miguel.

Basque: topographic name from landa ‘(large) field, prairie’. There is a place of this name in Araba/Álava province, Basque Country (Spain), and the surname may also be a habitational name from this place.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads in southwestern Norway, so named from the plural of land ‘land, territory’ (see Land 3).

variants.name: Lant
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Land   with devoicing of the final consonant.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Alabama Becomes a State
historicEvents.description: Alabama became the twenty-second state admitted to the Union on December 14, 1819.

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.
Historical Boundaries: 1836: DeKalb, Alabama, United States
In 1860, South Carolina quit the United States because its citizens were in favor of slavery and President Lincoln was not. The Civil War started a year later.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: A Treaty with Hawaii
historicEvents.description: In the Mid 1870s, The United States sought out the Kingdom of Hawaii to make a free trade agreement. The Treaty gave the Hawaiians access to the United States agricultural markets and it gave the United States a part of land which later became Pearl Harbor.

historicEvents.title: Liberian Exodus
historicEvents.description: April 21, 1878, around 206 African Americans boarde the ship Azor for Liberia.The cause for this is with the rise of political power, it makes life even harder for the African Americans.

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth Land was born on 10 June 1815, in Spartanburg, Spartanburg, South Carolina, United States, her father, Lewis Land, was 56 and her mother, Elizabeth Martha Ray, was 54. She married John Bailey on 15 July 1831, in South Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. She lived in Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, United States in 1870 and Walnut Hill, Lafayette, Arkansas, United States in 1879. She died on 16 March 1890, in Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, United States, at the age of 74, and was buried in Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth Land est née le 10 juin 1815, à Spartanburg, Spartanburg, Caroline du Sud, États-Unis, son père, Lewis Land, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Martha Ray, avait 54 ans. Elle a épousé John Bailey le 15 juillet 1831, à Caroline du Sud, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Elle a vécu à Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1870 et à Walnut Hill, Lafayette, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1879. Elle est décédée le 16 mars 1890, à Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, États-Unis, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth Land am 10. Juni 1815 in Spartanburg, Spartanburg, South Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lewis Land 56 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Martha Ray 54. Sie hat am 15. Juli 1831 in South Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten John Bailey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1870 hat sie in Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten und 1879 in Walnut Hill, Lafayette, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb am 16. März 1890 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth Land è nata il il 10 giugno 1815 nel Spartanburg, Spartanburg, Carolina del Sud, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Lewis Land, aveva 56 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Martha Ray, aveva 54 anni. Lei sposato John Bailey il 15 luglio 1831, nel Carolina del Sud, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 7 figlie. Vissuto a Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1870 e nel Walnut Hill, Lafayette, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1879. Morì il 16 marzo 1890, nel Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolta a Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Landが1815年6月10日にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州スパータンブルグスパータンブルグで生まれた時，父Lewis Landは56歳，母Elizabeth Martha Rayは54歳であった。1831年7月15日にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州でJohn Baileyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1870年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ランドルフロアノク トウンシュイップに，1879年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ラファイエットワルヌト ヒルに住んでいた。彼女は1890年3月16日にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ラウレンスラベンデン スプリングズで74歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ラウレンスラベンデン スプリングズジャンス セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arkansas, Un: 미국 아칸소 주 랜돌프 로노크 타운시프

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Land(이)가 1815년 6월 10일 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 스파르탠버그 스파르탠버그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lewis Land(은)는 56세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Martha Ray(은)는 54세였다. 그녀는 1831년 7월 15일 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주에서 John Bailey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1870년도에 미국 아칸소 주 랜돌프 로노크 타운시프에서, 1879년도에 미국 아칸소 주 라파예트 월누트 힐에서 살았다. 그녀는 1890년 3월 16일 미국 아칸소 주 로렌스 레이븐든 스프링스에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 아칸소 주 로렌스 레이븐든 스프링스 제네스 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth Land nasceu em 10 de junho de 1815, em Spartanburg, Spartanburg, Carolina do Sul, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Lewis Land, tinha 56 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Martha Ray, tinha 54 anos. Ela casou-se com John Bailey em 15 de julho de 1831, em Carolina do Sul, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela viveu em Roanoke Township, Randolph, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1870 e em Walnut Hill, Lafayette, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1879. Ela faleceu em 16 de março de 1890, em Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América, com 74 anos, e foi sepultada em Janes Cemetery, Ravenden Springs, Lawrence, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth Land родилась 10 июня 1815 г. в местности спартанбург, спартанбург, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Lewis Land было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Martha Ray, 54. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Bailey 15 июля 1831 г. в местности Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Она жила в местности роанок тоуншип, рандолф, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1870 году и в местности валнут хилл, лафеиетт, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1879 году. Она умерла 16 марта 1890 г. в местности равенден спрингс, ларенс, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности дженесь кеметири, равенден спрингс, ларенс, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты.
